title: Typhoon Bavi impacts across western Pacific region
summary: Typhoon Bavi swept across the western Pacific in July 2026, causing deaths and displacement across the Philippines, Taiwan, Japan, and China.

events.chapter_summary: Typhoon Bavi, one of the largest and strongest typhoons in decades, killed at least 15 people in Philippine landslides on Mindanao in early July 2026 while threatening Taiwan, Japan, and eastern China, prompting mass evacuations and flight cancellations across the region.

Typhoon Bavi made landfall in China's Zhejiang province on July 11 with winds of approximately 144 km/h, prompting the evacuation of nearly two million people, and was subsequently downgraded as it moved inland.
